metadata.name: pyformance
metadata.summary: Performance metrics, based on Coda Hale's Yammer metrics

variables.name: setup.install_requires
documentation: optional: ["blinker==1.2"]

classes.name: pyformance.stats.moving_average.ExpWeightedMovingAvg

methods.name: pyformance.stats.moving_average.ExpWeightedMovingAvg.__init__
documentation:
Create a new EWMA with a specific smoothing constant.

```
type period

C{int}

param period

the time in minutes it takes to reach a given significance level

type interval

C{int}

param interval

the expected tick interval, defaults to 5s
```

methods.name: pyformance.stats.moving_average.ExpWeightedMovingAvg.tick
documentation: Mark the passage of time and decay the current rate accordingly.

methods.name: pyformance.stats.moving_average.ExpWeightedMovingAvg._alpha
documentation:
Calculate the alpha based on the time since the last tick. This is
necessary because a single threaded Python program loses precision
under high load, so we can't assume a consistant I{EWMA.\_interval}.

```
type interval

C{float}

param interval

the interval we use to calculate the alpha
```

class_variables.name: pyformance.stats.moving_average.ExpWeightedMovingAvg.INTERVAL
documentation: seconds
An exponentially-weighted moving average.

classes.name: pyformance.stats.snapshot.Snapshot

get custom percentile

```
param percentile

float value between 0 and 1
```
This class is used by the histogram meter

classes.name: pyformance.meters.meter.Meter
documentation:
A meter metric which measures mean throughput and one-, five-, and fifteen-minute
exponentially-weighted moving average throughputs.

classes.name: pyformance.stats.samples.ExpDecayingSample

methods.name: pyformance.stats.samples.ExpDecayingSample.__init__
documentation:
Creates a new L\{ExponentiallyDecayingSample}.

```
type size

C{int}

param size

the number of samples to keep in the sampling reservoir

type alpha

C{float}

param alpha

the exponential decay factor; the higher this is, the more
biased the sample will be towards newer values

type clock

C{function}

param clock

the function used to return the current time, default to
seconds since the epoch; to be used with other time
units, or with the twisted clock for our testing purposes
```

methods.name: pyformance.stats.samples.ExpDecayingSample.update
documentation:
Adds a value to the sample.

```
type value

C{int} or C{float}

param value

the value to be added
```

class_variables.name: pyformance.stats.samples.ExpDecayingSample.RESCALE_THREASHOLD
documentation: 1 hour

An exponentially-decaying random sample of longs. Uses Cormode et al's forward-decaying priority reservoir sampling method to produce a statistically representative sample, exponentially biased towards newer entries. @see: Cormode et al. Forward Decay: A Practical Time Decay Model for Streaming Systems. ICDE '09: Proceedings of the 2009 IEEE International Conference on Data Engineering (2009)

classes.name: pyformance.stats.samples.SlidingTimeWindowSample

methods.name: pyformance.stats.samples.SlidingTimeWindowSample.__init__
documentation:
Creates a SlidingTimeWindowSample.

```
param window

the length of the time window in seconds

param clock

clock.time() is called to get the current time as seconds
since the epoch.
```
A sample of measurements made in a sliding time window.

classes.name: pyformance.meters.timer.Timer

methods.name: pyformance.meters.timer.Timer.time
documentation:
Parameters will be sent to signal, if fired.
Returns a timer context instance which can be used from a with-statement.
Without with-statement you have to call the stop method on the context

A timer metric which aggregates timing durations and provides duration statistics, plus
throughput statistics via Meter and Histogram.

classes.name: pyformance.registry.MetricsRegistry

documentation: Creates a new L{MetricsRegistry} instance.

methods.name: pyformance.registry.MetricsRegistry.add
documentation:
Use this method to manually add custom metric instances to the registry
which are not created with their constructor's default arguments,
e.g. Histograms with a different size.

```
param key

name of the metric

type key

C{str}

param metric

instance of Histogram, Meter, Gauge, Timer or Counter
```

methods.name: pyformance.registry.MetricsRegistry.counter
documentation:

return

methods.name: pyformance.registry.MetricsRegistry.dump_metrics
documentation:
Formats all of the metrics and returns them as a dict.

```
return

C{list} of C{dict} of metrics
```

A single interface used to gather metrics on a service. It keeps track of
all the relevant Counters, Meters, Histograms, and Timers. It does not have
a reference back to its service. The service would create a
L{MetricsRegistry} to manage all of its metrics tools.

classes.name: pyformance.registry.RegexRegistry
documentation:
A single interface used to gather metrics on a service. This class uses a regex to combine
measures that match a pattern. For example, if you have a REST API, instead of defining
a timer for each method, you can use a regex to capture all API calls and group them.
A pattern like '^/api/(?P<model>)/\d+/(?P<verb>)?$' will group and measure the following:
    /api/users/1 -> users
    /api/users/1/edit -> users/edit
    /api/users/2/edit -> users/edit
